The **2015 New Zealand rugby league tour of England** was a tour by the [New Zealand national rugby league team](/wiki/New_Zealand_national_rugby_league_team "New Zealand national rugby league team"). The [New Zealand national rugby league team](/wiki/New_Zealand_national_rugby_league_team "New Zealand national rugby league team") played a match against the [Leeds Rhinos](/wiki/Leeds_Rhinos "Leeds Rhinos") and competed in a three match test series against [England](/wiki/England_national_rugby_league_team "England national rugby league team") for the [Baskerville Shield](/wiki/Baskerville_Shield "Baskerville Shield"), losing 2-1.

## Background

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2015_New_Zealand_rugby_league_tour_of_England&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Background")]

| [RLIF World Rankings](/wiki/RLIF_World_Rankings "RLIF World Rankings") * [v](/wiki/Template:RLIF_World_Rankings "Template:RLIF World Rankings") * [t](/wiki/Template_talk:RLIF_World_Rankings "Template talk:RLIF World Rankings") * [e](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Template:RLIF_World_Rankings "Special:EditPage/Template:RLIF World Rankings") | | | |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Official Rankings as of May 2015**[[1]](#cite_note-1) | | | |
| Rank | Change | Team | Points |
| 1 | Increase | [New Zealand](/wiki/New_Zealand_national_rugby_league_team "New Zealand national rugby league team") | 949.00 |
| 3 | Steady | [England](/wiki/England_national_rugby_league_team "England national rugby league team") | 485.00 |

New Zealand had originally intended to host a tour against [France](/wiki/France_national_rugby_league_team "France national rugby league team"), while Australia was speculated to host a [Great Britain Lions](/wiki/Great_Britain_Lions "Great Britain Lions") tour. However the [Australian Rugby League Commission](/wiki/Australian_Rugby_League_Commission "Australian Rugby League Commission") declined to host a tour and as an alternative the [Rugby Football League](/wiki/Rugby_Football_League "Rugby Football League") invited the [New Zealand Rugby League](/wiki/New_Zealand_Rugby_League "New Zealand Rugby League") to tour Great Britain for a three match series.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

On 25 February 2015, the three test match venues were confirmed with Hull's [KC Stadium](/wiki/KC_Stadium "KC Stadium") hosting game one on 1 November, the [London Olympic Stadium](/wiki/Olympic_Stadium_(London) "Olympic Stadium (London)") hosting the second test on 7 November and the final test match being held at Wigan's [DW Stadium](/wiki/DW_Stadium "DW Stadium") on 14 November.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

The match against Leeds was the first time a [Super League](/wiki/Super_League "Super League") side had played a touring side since [2002](/wiki/2002_New_Zealand_rugby_league_tour_of_Great_Britain_and_France "2002 New Zealand rugby league tour of Great Britain and France"), when the Kiwis played matches against [Hull F.C.](/wiki/Hull_F.C. "Hull F.C.") and [St. Helens](/wiki/St_Helens_R.F.C. "St Helens R.F.C."). It was the first time that Leeds played New Zealand since 1992. The match celebrated the 125th anniversary of [Headingley](/wiki/Headingley_Rugby_Stadium "Headingley Rugby Stadium"). Leeds has defeated New Zealand once in eleven matches between the two teams.[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Squad

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2015_New_Zealand_rugby_league_tour_of_England&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Squad")]

### Training squad

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2015_New_Zealand_rugby_league_tour_of_England&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Training squad")]

An initial training squad was named on 8 September 2015 and added to as teams were eliminated from the [NRL finals](/wiki/2015_NRL_season#Finals_series "2015 NRL season"). [Kieran Foran](/wiki/Kieran_Foran "Kieran Foran") was among those initially named but he later withdrew due to injury, joining [Shaun Johnson](/wiki/Shaun_Johnson "Shaun Johnson"), [Thomas Leuluai](/wiki/Thomas_Leuluai "Thomas Leuluai"), [Manu Vatuvei](/wiki/Manu_Vatuvei "Manu Vatuvei"), [Dallin Watene-Zelezniak](/wiki/Dallin_Watene-Zelezniak "Dallin Watene-Zelezniak") and [Jared Warea-Hargreaves](/wiki/Jared_Warea-Hargreaves "Jared Warea-Hargreaves") as being unavailable for the tour.[[6]](#cite_note-6) Skipper [Simon Mannering](/wiki/Simon_Mannering "Simon Mannering") was also later made unavailable for the test series due to injury.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

### Final squad

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2015_New_Zealand_rugby_league_tour_of_England&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Final squad")]

The final touring squad of 23 players was named on 8 October.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

On 10 October, [Jason Taumalolo](/wiki/Jason_Taumalolo "Jason Taumalolo") withdrew from the touring 23-man squad due to a knee injury. He was replaced by [Manaia Cherrington](/wiki/Manaia_Cherrington "Manaia Cherrington").[[13]](#cite_note-13) [Curtis Rona](/wiki/Curtis_Rona "Curtis Rona") left the tour after the second test match, returning to Australia to be present for the birth of his daughter.[[14]](#cite_note-14)

With the win, England retained the [Baskerville Shield](/wiki/Baskerville_Shield "Baskerville Shield"). [Isaac Liu](/wiki/Isaac_Liu "Isaac Liu") and [Gerard Beale](/wiki/Gerard_Beale "Gerard Beale") were 18th and 19th man respectively for New Zealand.[[29]](#cite_note-29)

## Aftermath

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2015_New_Zealand_rugby_league_tour_of_England&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Aftermath")]

England's captain, Sean O'Loughlin was awarded the [George Smith](/wiki/George_William_Smith_(sportsman) "George William Smith (sportsman)") Medal as player of the series.

England's retained the Baskerville Shield, 8 years after it was last contested during the [2007 All Golds Tour](/wiki/2007_All_Golds_Tour "2007 All Golds Tour").[[30]](#cite_note-30)
